posts.content:
Food for thought:

If Evloev fought in July, it would represent the quickest turnaround of his UFC career.  If history can serve as a guide whatsoever, he won't fight again until January 2027.  It's been an average of 9+ months between fights for him.  He's coming off his first five rounder in almost 8 years where he got a little dinged up, and his next fight is the biggest of his career.

posts.author: B7L7
posts.content:
[Predictions]

@Aceofheartss88 let me get home from work, I'll answer to this in like 30 minutes.

posts.author: B7L7
posts.content:
[Predictions]

@Aceofheartss88 alright, here I am, so:

1. I remember watching the first fight between Conor and Max which was 2013 (I hate assuming, but since you assumed I know nothing about fighting, I’m automatically going to assume you were 5 years old in 2013, so deal with it😂😎). I haven’t rewatched it since but remember Conor wrestling a lot. Max is levels above that version of himself, and his volume is overwhelming for most guys. Conor McGregor is about to be 38, and between his age, number of serious training sessions or extended periods of training since 2021 that you can count on one hand, and the ring rust, he’s a shell of a fighter he once was, and isn’t a counter sniper.

2. Puncher’s chance is the most overused and overrated term in MMA. If anything, I’d argue Conor has more of a punchers chance against Charles than against Max. Obviously Max is less durable now, but he’s also less chinny than Charles and has way better boxing defense. Charles is super stationary, has dangerous offensive muay thai, but he’s very susceptible to boxing counters (even though I already said Conor’s not going to be a super quick counter striker). Charles gets tagged on the feet in every fight he has. Another thing, he’s an elite submission grappler, but not an elite wrestler. I don’t know what you’ve been watching but he won’t be able to take down Conor at will. Prime Khabib wasn’t able to do that against coked up Conor, and someone who’s 2 levels below Khabib in pure wrestling shouldn’t be able to do that, even though it will be easier as Conor gets more tired.

Now I still think both guys smash Conor in this day and age, I just wanted to give you educated reasoning that you don’t have to agree with, but I hope you can respect other people’s opinions without automatically having to be rude towards them, like that one fool over here who called another guy’s mom a ***** because he bet on a different fight outcome.

* Edited at 04.28.2026,  9:49 AM ET *

posts.author: Gilpin
posts.content: @B7L7 I can see what you're getting at, but lets be realistic. A lot of people act like Conor is a celebrity like CM Punk who is naive to think they can fight in the UFC and they straight up give him no chance in a fight with any ranked opponent. We have to remember that he was legitimately the best striker in the UFC at one point and that skill doesn't leave you, I think his biggest flaw in a fight with Max is his endurance and chin, but striking wise McGregor still has the skill to make it competitive. That being said, if the fight were to happen... give me Max by round 3 TKO

posts.author: IlikeMMAandboobs
posts.content:
Saint-Denis vs. Pimblett
Benoit Saint-Denis, TKO, R3
Benoit has power in his hands and a good grappling: he has a 100% finish rate. Paddy has a chin for sure, but let's remember he got dropped by Gaethje a few times, with the difference that Benoit can engage on the ground too, unlike Gaethje, whose ground-and-pound isn't as good as the Benoit's one. I think Paddy will end up on the ground in the later rounds and get TKO'ed. Could be one of the most amazing fights of the last years.
